perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall cooperate with and aid the Disclosing Party in mitigating and preventing the unauthorized use and disclosure and any furtherance thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may cooperate with and aid the Disclosing Party in mitigating and preventing the unauthorized use and disclosure and any furtherance thereof. Such aid is not mandatory.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' and adding 'Such aid is not mandatory' in Section 4 introduces a contradiction. Originally, the Recipient *must* cooperate in preventing unauthorized use. Now, cooperation is optional. This directly contradicts the initial, stronger obligation, creating uncertainty in section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOTICE OF UNAUTHORIZED USE. Recipient shall provide written notice to Disclosing Party without undue delay of any misuse or mis appropriation of Confidential Information which may come to the attention of Recipient . The Recipient may cooperate with and aid the Disclosing Party in mitigating and preventing the unauthorized use and disclosure and any furtherance thereof. Such aid is not mandatory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall' to 'may' and adds 'Such aid is not mandatory'. The original text in the contradicted section includes the statement 'Recipient shall provide written notice'. The change allows the Recipient to optionally cooperate, directly contradicting the original obligation to provide written notice without undue delay. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because one section mandates action while the other permits inaction regarding unauthorized use.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosing Party may serve written request on Recipient for return or destruction of its Confidential Information at any time up to six (6) months after the termination or expiry of this Agreement and Recipient shall, within thirty (30) days of such request or termination, return to the Disclosing Party (or its designees) or certify as destroyed all Confidential Information, in whatever form, including written or electronically recorded information and al l copies thereof (other than copies retained in automatic back -up and archive systems), provided however that Recipient shall be entitled to retain one copy of the Confidential Information with its legal counsel or other appropriate corporate representativ e to evidence the exchange of information hereunder and in connection with legal or statutory requirements .
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing Party may serve written request on Recipient for return or destruction of its Confidential Information at any time up to six (6) months after the termination or expiry of this Agreement and Recipient may, within thirty (30) days of such request or termination, return to the Disclosing Party (or its designees) or certify as destroyed all Confidential Information. Recipient can opt to keep all copies of Confidential information for future use.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' in Section 6 and adding 'Recipient can opt to keep all copies of Confidential information for future use' creates a direct contradiction. The initial obligation to return or destroy is now optional, and Recipient is expressly allowed to keep the information. The first half of the statement imposes destruction or return, whereas the second half permits the Recipient to ignore such action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REPRODUCTION AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall not be copied or reproduced without the Disclosing Party’s prior written permission, except for such copies as may reasonably be required for the purpose as set forth in Section I of this Agreem ent. Disclosing Party may serve written request on Recipient for return or destruction of its Confidential Information at any time up to six (6) months after the termination or expiry of this Agreement and Recipient may, within thirty (30) days of such request or termination, return to the Disclosing Party (or its designees) or certify as destroyed all Confidential Information. Recipient can opt to keep all copies of Confidential information for future use. All such retained copies shall remain subject to the use and disclosure restrictions in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text and contradicted text both state that the Recipient 'shall' return or destroy confidential information after termination. The changed text modifies this to 'may' return or destroy, and explicitly allows the Recipient to keep copies 'for future use'. This directly contradicts the original requirement to return or destroy the information, and the contradicted text that says 'The Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall not be copied or reproduced without the Disclosing Party’s prior written permission'. Therefore, this is a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
